title: Fix type operator for interface signals (#6050)
body:
This used to work:
```
localparam type intf_type = type(intf_pin.foo);
```
Now I'm getting:
```
%Error: Internal Error: t/t_param_type6.v:36:33: ../V3AstNodes.cpp:844: REFDTYPE not linked to type
                                               : ... note: In instance 't.sub_eight'
-node: REFDTYPE 0x555556ddf770 <e966> {f36bh} @dt=0@ -> UNLINKED
   36 |     localparam type intf_type = type(intf_pin.foo);
      |                                 ^~~~
```
because during `param()` we have this:
```
    1:2: PARAMTYPEDTYPE 0x555556e72b60 <e971> {f36av} u3=0x154 @dt=0@  intf_type -> REQUIREDTYPE 0x555556dd5e40 <e968> {f36av} @dt=0@
    1:2:1: REQUIREDTYPE 0x555556dd5e40 <e968> {f36av} @dt=0@
    1:2:1:1: REFDTYPE 0x555556ddf770 <e966> {f36bh} @dt=0@ -> UNLINKED
    1:2:1:1:1: VARXREF 0x555556d4fa40 <e1014> {f36bv} @dt=0@  foo [RV] <- .=intf_pin VAR 0x555556d45e60 <e614> {f10ao} @dt=0@  foo [VSTATIC]  VAR
```
also if I look at the `foo` Var, it has the wrong (default) parameter type.

I assume this is because `param()` is happening before `linkDotParamed()`.  Can I defer some or all param'ing until after the dots are linked?  If so, is it possible that we'll have to repeatedly do these steps until it stabilizes?
